methods.description: This method involves obtaining and analyzing CMIP5 data for piControl, lgm, and abrupt4×CO2 experiments to calculate equilibrium climate sensitivities and radiative feedbacks.

steps.description: CMIP5 data were obtained for piControl, lgm, and abrupt4×CO2 experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain CMIP5 data for specified experiments

steps.description: The latter were averaged over years 101-150.
Short-wave cloud radiative feedbacks in PMIP2, PMIP3, and abrupt4×CO2 simulations are calculated using the approximate partial radiative perturbation method.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate SW cloud radiative feedbacks using APRP method

steps.description: Equilibrium climate sensitivities for the CMIP5 models are derived using the method of Gregory et al. [2004].

methods.description: This method decomposes short-wave radiative forcing and feedbacks in climate models using the APRP method and calculates long-wave fluxes and greenhouse gas feedbacks.

steps.description: The short-wave (SW) radiative forcing and feedbacks in each model are decomposed using the APRP method of Taylor et al. [2007].
steps.searchable_summary: decompose SW radiative forcing using APRP method

steps.description: The LW cloud term is calculated as the difference between TOA all-sky and clear-sky fluxes.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate LW cloud term from TOA fluxes

steps.description: The greenhouse gas forcing is calculated from the 2×CO2 (for PMIP2 models) or 4×CO2 (for CMIP5 models) greenhouse gas forcing, scaled to the LGM GHG change.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate GHG forcing scaled to LGM change
